news.summary: After President Donald Trump implemented a tariff plan criticized as stagflationary, key Federal Reserve figures, such as Mary Daly, President of the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco, hinted at a potential delay in further rate cuts. Daly, during a discussion at Brigham Young University, expressed satisfaction with the current interest rate levels. She noted that the interest rate had been lowered by 100 basis points the previous year, positioning the policy in a modestly restrictive stance. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: San Fran Fed's Mary Daly urges caution on rate cuts as Trump's tariff plan criticized. Daly comfortable with current rates after 100 basis point cut last year.

news.summary: FB Financial (NYSE: FBK) is scheduled to release its quarterly earnings on Monday, April 14, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.81 for the company. Investors are eagerly awaiting the announcement, eagerly anticipating that FB Financial might exceed expectations and provide optimistic guidance for the upcoming quarter. Details for new investors can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: FB Financial (NYSE: FBK) to release earnings report on 2025-04-14. Analysts expect EPS of $0.81. Investors eager for positive guidance.

news.summary: The recent increase in U.S. Treasury yields has caused the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) swap spreads to widen, indicating a potential liquidity crisis. SOFR is an interest rate benchmark reflecting overnight cash borrowing costs. The surge in SOFR swap spread is attributed to the considerable rise in 10-year Treasury bond yields, climbing from 3.87% on April 4 to 4.44% presently. Cathie Wood of Ark Invest believes the only solution lies in government and Federal Reserve interventions. Wood proposes a "Mar-a-Lago Accord" trade agreement alongside aggressive Fed measures. Zerohedge also highlights the worrying liquidity issues in the U.S. banking system. The severity of the situation underscores the need for immediate action.
news.headline: Cathie Wood warns of liquidity issues as SOFR swap spreads widen on spiking Treasury yields. 10-year bond yields up to 4.44%, requiring government and Fed intervention for containment. Mar-a-Lago Accord proposed by Wood for resolution.

news.summary: Applied Digital (NASDAQ: APLD) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on April 14, 2025, with analysts forecasting an earnings per share (EPS) of $-0.11. In the previous quarter, the company exceeded expectations by $0.08, resulting in a 1.99% drop in share price the following day. The historical performance of Applied Digital shows varying EPS estimates and actual figures, along with corresponding price changes. As of April 10, the company's shares were trading at $5.13, marking a 111.33% increase over the past 52 weeks. Analysts' views on Applied Digital are crucial for investors to gauge market sentiments and expectations. Further details on this news article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Applied Digital (NASDAQ: APLD) is set to release EPS of $-0.11 on 2025-04-14. Recent performance saw share prices drop 1.99%. Analysts are optimistic for exceeding expectations and providing positive guidance. Stock trading at $5.13, up 111.33% in the last 52 weeks. Investor optimism prevails.

news.summary: Renowned investor Bill Ackman has linked the recent extreme volatility in rates, currencies, and equities to "forced selling" by heavily leveraged market players, rather than fundamental economic changes. The billionaire pointed this out in a recent post, stating that many are misinterpreting short-term market fluctuations and confusing technical sell-offs with actual economic or policy shifts. Ackman emphasized the excessive leverage in the market, noting the allowed leverage of up to 10:1 for stocks and a striking 100:1 for Treasuries and currencies. He explained that this heightened leverage exposes traders to margin calls and compelled position closures in times of market tension, leading to escalated and potentially deceptive price movements. Ackman expressed his belief that the recent sharp swings in these assets are more likely due to highly leveraged participants. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bill Ackman blames market chaos on forced selling by leveraged traders, not economic shifts. High leverage ratios contribute to volatile price swings.

news.summary: Stock futures in the U.S. were turbulent on Wednesday after President Trump's announcement of a 104% tariff on Chinese imports. The tariffs were set to go into effect at 12:01 a.m. ET. Despite the escalating trade tensions, a report by the U.S. Chamber of Commerce Foundation indicated that many U.S. companies still see advantages in doing business in China. Major indices had significant drops from recent highs, with the S&P 500 down 18.95%, Nasdaq 100 down 23.10%, and Dow Jones down 16.48%. Treasury bond yields stood at 4.35% for the 10-year and 3.80% for the two-year. Markets indicated a 58.3% probability of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates. In premarket trading, futures for the Dow Jones were down 0.06%, S&P 500 up 0.22%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.64%, and Russell 2000 down 0.17%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF saw increases, with SPY up 0.30% to $497.98 and QQQ up 0.63% to $418.70. In the previous session, U.S. stocks experienced a broad decline, led by the consumer discretionary, materials, and energy sectors following the tariff announcement. Individual tech giants like Apple, Tesla, Amazon, and Nvidia also saw significant drops. Walgreens reported positive earnings, but small business optimism hit a low point. The Dow Jones fell 320 points to 37,645.59.
news.headline: US stock futures mixed pre-market as uncertainty grows post-Trump's 104% tariff on China. Major indices down from recent highs: S&P 500 -18.95%, Nasdaq 100 -23.10%, Dow Jones -16.48%. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged in May. S&P 500 ETF up 0.30%, Nasdaq 100 ETF up 0.63%. Consumer discretionary, materials, energy sectors declining heavily. Walgreens reports strong earnings, small business optimism down. Full details on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In March, producer input prices experienced a larger-than-expected decline, signaling a wider deceleration in consumer inflation from the previous day. Despite early tariff impacts not showing up in wholesale costs, the Producer Price Index (PPI) only increased by 2.7% year-over-year, a significant drop from the previous 3.2%. Released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, this data was well below the anticipated 3.3% forecasted by economists. Monthly figures showed a 0.4% decrease in wholesale prices for March, following a revised 0.1% uptick in February and missing the projected 0.2% increase. The decrease was mainly due to a significant decline in goods prices, responsible for over 70% of the final demand index drop. Final demand goods prices saw a 0.9% decrease, the most significant contributor to the decline.
news.headline: Producer inflation cools sharply in March as input prices fall more than expected. PPI increased by 2.7% YoY, below economist forecast of 3.3%. Monthly basis shows a 0.4% decline in wholesale prices. Good prices accounted for over 70% drop in the final demand index.

news.summary: The economic tensions between the US and China have deepened with China announcing a 125% tariff increase on US goods in response to Washington's tariff hike. However, the digital asset market, specifically Bitcoin, has remained stable with Bitcoin trading above $82,000 and total crypto market capitalization at $2.6 trillion. Analysts believe this stability reflects a shift in investor behavior, with long-term holders accumulating while shorter-term participants exit the market. Industry leaders caution against interpreting this calm as a positive sign, noting that Bitcoin is increasingly seen as an alternative to the traditional financial system, similar to gold. The article highlights how Bitcoin's stability may be due to this perception, rather than mere complacency.
news.headline: Bitcoin and stablecoins benefit from trade war tensions, market stable at $2.6 trillion cap, BTC trading above $82,000.

news.summary: XRP is currently trading around $1.99 after a week of dramatic price movements in the cryptocurrency market. Despite a wave of bullish news, the altcoin market remains mixed, with XRP holding steady above the $2 psychological level. The token experienced a 15% surge earlier in the week after President Trump's tariff announcements. XRP has since consolidated around $2, with potential resistance at $2.09 and support at $1.93. Technical indicators suggest another breakout may be imminent, with a possible target of $2.38 if it closes above $2.04. Standard Chartered predicts that XRP will surpass Ethereum in the near future. Overall, traders are monitoring the market for potential entry points amidst geopolitical uncertainty.
news.headline: XRP remains steady around $1.99 after 15% weekly surge amid bullish long-term news, may gear up for breakout. Standard Chartered predicts XRP to overtake Ethereum.

news.summary: Economist Craig Shapiro has raised concerns about a potential "market dysfunction" in the U.S. Treasury market, with the possibility of the "Fed put" being activated soon. Less than 3 days after his warning, Shapiro observed signs of UST market dysfunction, possibly triggered by factors like forced deleveraging and foreign repatriation following the U.S. imposing 104% tariffs on China. This has led to a rise in 10-year and 30-year Treasury yields despite expectations of lower yields during market turmoil. Moody's Analytics Chief Economist Mark Zandi suggested that the yield increase may be due to hedge funds selling Treasuries to cover stock position margin calls, while some experts speculate China selling U.S. Treasuries in response to tariffs. Shapiro recommended investing in gold in anticipation of the Fed's reaction to the evolving market conditions.
news.headline: Economist warns of potential Treasury market dysfunction, signals Fed intervention; suggests buying gold as strategy in response to market uncertainties.

news.summary: Stocks in the U.S. saw an upswing today, with the Nasdaq Composite rising by over 50 points. The Dow started the day 0.19% higher at 39,669.09, while the NASDAQ increased by 0.36% to reach 16,446.23. The S&P 500 also experienced growth, up by 0.22% at 5,279.73. Materials sector shares surged by 1.5%, while real estate shares dipped by 0.3%. In notable news, JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) exceeded expectations in its first-quarter financial report, with earnings per share at $5.07, surpassing the estimated $4.62 per share. Quarterly sales were also better than anticipated at $46.014 billion. For more details, visit a financial news website.
news.headline: Nasdaq up 50 pts; JPMorgan beats expectations with $5.07 EPS and $46.014 billion sales in Q1. Materials shares surged.

news.summary: Wall Street is bracing for the March inflation report following market volatility amid tariff tensions, which could impact rate cuts. Federal Reserve Chair noted no rush for cuts amidst uncertainty due to tariffs. A soft inflation print could lead to a more dovish stance, providing hope for markets facing swift selloffs. The S&P 500 has dropped over 10% since Trump's tariff announcement and currently sits 18% below its February levels. This uncertainty is being closely monitored by investors awaiting the CPI data on Thursday to gauge potential economic impacts.
news.headline: Wall Street awaits crucial March inflation report, possibly leading to rate cuts amid trade tensions. S&P 500 dropped 10% since tariff announcement. Fed Chair mentions no rush for rate cuts. Fed cornered by sticky inflation stirring investor sentiment. A soft inflation print could offer hope in turbulent times.

news.summary: Bitcoin is currently trading around $83,000 after bouncing from lows around $78,953, marking a 4% gain. This increase is attributed to a new wave of institutional and government interest in the crypto market. The Florida House Insurance and Banking Subcommittee approved a bill, HB 487, that would allow the state to invest up to $1.5 billion in Bitcoin. This marks a historic moment as Florida becomes the first U.S. state to advance a Bitcoin reserve bill without opposition. This bipartisan decision has surprised many, positioning Florida at the forefront of an emerging trend among states to build digital asset reserves. The bill's sponsor, Representative Webster Barnaby, believes this legislation will place Florida in a leading position in the digital asset space.
news.headline: Bitcoin rises to $83K as Florida passes Bitcoin reserve bill allocating $1.5 billion, first state to advance bill unanimously.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, in Clermont-Ferrand, France, CARBIOS (Euronext Growth Paris: ALCRB) released its operating and financial outcomes for the fiscal year 2024. The figures from December 31, 2024, were sanctioned by the company's Board of Directors during their gathering on April 10, 2025. CARBIOS, known for pioneering biological technologies in reshaping the life cycle of plastic and textiles, shared key details about its performance in the reported period.
news.headline: CARBIOS announces fiscal-year 2024 financial results; financial statements approved on April 10, 2025.

news.summary: The US sustainable aviation fuel market is projected to reach $6.97 billion in 2030, up from $0.86 billion in 2024, with a growth rate of 46.8%. This growth is fueled by factors such as increasing concerns about carbon emissions, government initiatives to reduce emissions through tax credits, and airlines like United Air, KLM, and JetBlue incorporating sustainable aviation fuel in blends with conventional fuels. The market is segmented by fuel type into biofuel, hydrogen fuel, power-to-liquid, and gas-to-liquid, with biofuels being the dominant segment in 2024. Key players in the industry include World Energy, LLC, Fulcrum BioEnergy, Gevo, Red Rock Biofuels, WasteFuel, and Prometheus Fuels. United Airlines' successful commercial flight using 100% sustainable aviation fuel has demonstrated the technology's potential and contributed to its wider acceptance.
news.headline: US Sustainable Aviation Fuel Market to hit $6.97B by 2030, growing at 46.8% CAGR. Biofuel leads segment. Notable players incl. World Energy, Fulcrum BioEnergy, Gevo. Airlines like United Air, KLM, JetBlue integrating sustainable av fuel. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Solana (SOL) surged by more than 6% on Friday morning, surpassing $120.00, benefiting from the positive momentum earlier in the week amidst increasing institutional trust and ongoing macroeconomic instability. The latest development driving this growth is a significant investment in SOL by real estate tech company Janover Inc, who acquired 44,158 SOL valued at around $5 million as part of its treasury diversification strategy. This purchase has increased Janover's total SOL holdings to 83,084 SOL, equivalent to approximately $9.6 million, as stated in a press release issued by the company on Friday morning. Janover Inc has confirmed it will promptly start staking the newly procured SOL, aligning with other corporations aiming to generate returns and engage in the ecosystem through validator nodes. This acquisition signifies the second notable investment in Solana by Janover in less than a week.
news.headline: Solana (SOL) jumps 6% to $120.00 with Janover adding $5M, doubling holdings to 83,084 SOL worth $9.6M. Janover to stake newly acquired SOL.
Tryg A/S released their Q1 2025 interim report, showcasing significant growth in key financial metrics.

news.summary: President Donald Trump has implemented a significant increase in tariffs on Chinese imports, now totaling at least 145%. This includes a 125% reciprocal tariff rise in addition to the existing 20% duties. In response, China has retaliated by imposing a 125% tariff on imports from the U.S. This escalation in the ongoing trade war has raised concerns about its impact on various companies. A recent report by Strategy Risks has identified the most exposed American companies based on criteria such as Business Fundamentals, Partnerships and politics, Regional Issues, Supply Chain, and Capacity. Ford Motor Co. (NYSE: F) has been identified as the most overexposed to China, scoring high in all criteria, particularly in Regional Issues. This development underscores the potential challenges facing companies with significant exposure to the Chinese market amid the escalating trade tensions.
news.headline: Trump increased tariffs on Chinese imports by minimum of 145%, leading to trade war with China. 10 companies, including Ford, Tesla, Apple, Disney, are most exposed to potential impacts of ongoing trade war. Ford ranked highest in overexposure to China based on various criteria like Business Fundamentals, Partnerships, Regional Issues.

news.summary: Bruker Corporation (NASDAQ: BRKR) has announced a preliminary revenue range for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025, of $795 million to $800 million, significantly surpassing the consensus forecast of $758.85 million. This marks a 10-11% increase year-over-year from $721.70 million. The company specializes in manufacturing scientific instruments for molecular and materials research, as well as industrial and applied analysis. The estimated revenue range suggests a reported growth of approximately 10% compared to the previous year, with low double-digit percentage growth in constant exchange rates. Bruker also anticipates adjusted organic revenue growth in the first quarter.
news.headline: Bruker Corporation (NASDAQ: BRKR) reports Q1 revenue of $795-800 million, up 10-11% YoY from $721.70 million.

news.summary: A significant shift in private investing is currently unfolding, expecting to significantly impact real estate as tokenization gains traction in 2025. Tokenization, previously viewed as just a buzzword in blockchain, is now being applied to traditional asset classes like real estate. This paves the way for new opportunities for savvy investors, allowing property ownership to be converted into digital tokens on a blockchain. This allows properties to be divided into hundreds or thousands of tradable shares, enabling investors to purchase fractions of assets similar to buying stocks or ETFs. The trend is already progressing, with tokenized multifamily properties offering quarterly distributions and commercial real estate deals being fractionalized for as little as $500 by 2025. Secondary markets are also emerging as part of this transformative wave.
news.headline: By 2025, tokenization is transforming real estate investing, allowing investors to buy fractions of assets like stocks.

news.summary: Stellantis N.V. (NYSE: STLA) shares are experiencing a decrease in trading on Friday following the company's announcement of a 9% year-over-year decline in shipments to approximately 1.2 million units in the quarter. The drop is attributed to reduced production in North America due to extended holiday downtime in January and in Enlarged Europe because of product transitions and decreased volumes of light commercial vehicles (LCVs). However, the first quarter of 2025 showed commercial advancement with the unveiling of various new and revamped models, such as the Citroën C3 Aircross, Opel Frontera, Fiat Grande Panda, and Ram 2500 and 3500 heavy-duty trucks.
news.headline: Stellantis Q1 shipments drop 9% to 1.2M units due to production issues in NA and EU. New models introduced.
Unity Bancorp announces $11.6M in quarterly earnings.

news.summary: According to economists, the decrease in U.S. manufacturing over the past few decades was primarily attributed to the rapid pace of change rather than free trade. This significant decline saw a substantial drop in manufacturing output, leading to a loss of numerous jobs and economic repercussions across various sectors. The lack of adequate time for industries to adapt to evolving technologies and market conditions played a crucial role in this shift. As a result, the U.S. experienced a notable decrease in its manufacturing base, impacting the economy and workforce significantly.
news.headline: Economists: U.S. manufacturing decline not due to free trade, but rapid change. Important lesson on adjustment without time.

news.summary: US stock futures rebounded on Friday morning after a weak Thursday session, with major benchmark index futures trading in positive territory during premarket hours. Annual inflation rate fell to a six-month low of 2.4% in March, down from 2.8% in February and below the expected 2.6%. The Consumer Price Index contracted by 0.1% monthly, marking the lowest reading since May 2020. 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.40% and the two-year bond was at 3.80%. Markets pricing a 61.6% likelihood of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged. Futures Change: Dow Jones 0.61%, S&P 500 0.49%, Nasdaq 100 1.05%, Russell 2000 0.67%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust up 0.62% to $524.58 and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF down 0.71% to $446.18. In Thursday's session, the Nasdaq 100 index was 17.46% lower than its previous high and the S&P 500 index was down 14.31% from its record. Energy, information technology, and communication services sectors led Thursday's market decline. Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla were significant decliners. Full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US stock futures bounce back, poised for higher open. Fed likely to cut rates on June 18. Inflation at 2.4%, CPI down by 0.1%. Major indices futures up in premarket: Dow (+0.61%), S&P 500 (+0.49%), Nasdaq 100 (+1.05%), Russell 2000 (+0.67%). Treasury bond yields: 10-year at 4.40%, two-year at 3.80%. Consumer staples up, tech, energy, comms down. Notable stock decliners include Apple (-4%), Nvidia (-6%), Tesla (-7%). Nasdaq 100 down 17.46% from previous high. FedWatch tool projects unchanged rates.

news.summary: The article discusses the potential impact of a dovish shift by the Federal Reserve, influenced by disinflationary pressures caused by President Donald Trump's tariff policies, on institutional investor sentiment towards cryptocurrencies. The U.S. economic landscape has been strained by tariffs, including a recent 125% tariff retaliation from China, disrupting global supply chains. Despite this, the crypto market, particularly Bitcoin, has shown resilience. Experts suggest that a dovish Fed with lower interest rates could lead to increased capital inflows into cryptocurrencies as investors seek higher returns in a low-yield environment. The potential for disinflationary pressures arising from Trump's tariffs could spark renewed institutional interest in cryptocurrencies as traditional assets become less appealing. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Fed's dovish shift due to Trump's tariff policies could boost institutional interest in Bitcoin. Market remains stable despite global economic uncertainty. Experts predict surge in crypto investments in a low-yield environment. Potential for institutional interest in digital assets grows as traditional assets become less appealing. A positive shift in institutional sentiment towards cryptocurrencies expected among low-yield assets in a looser monetary environment.

news.summary: Uxin, a Chinese used car market player, recently secured a funding boost of $7.5 million from Dida, adding to the $27 million infusion it received from Nio last month. The company reported strong growth in third-quarter sales and plans to expand to Wuhan and Zhengzhou. The company is focusing on the used car segment, as the new car market faces challenges due to a slowing economy. Uxin is racing to build its superstore network to operate profitably despite cash flow issues. This recent funding will help Uxin continue its growth trajectory.
news.headline: Uxin receives $7.5 million funding from Dida, $27 million from Nio. Plans to expand superstores in China, facing cash shortages.

news.summary: In Montreal on April 11, 2025, Aya Gold & Silver Inc. (TSX: AYA; OTCQX: AYASF) unveiled that for the initial quarter of 2025, the company achieved a silver production of 1.07 million ounces (1.07M oz) at its Zgounder Silver Mine in Morocco.
news.headline: Aya Gold & Silver reports record Q1-2025 production of 1.07M oz of silver at Zgounder Silver Mine in Morocco.

news.summary: Ursula von der Leyen aims to position the EU at the forefront of any potential reshaping of the global trading system. She is working towards ensuring the EU plays a significant role in shaping the future of international trade.
news.headline: Europe sees opportunity in Trump's trade disruption, aiming for EU to lead in new global trade system under Ursula von der Leyen.
Innventure releases 2024 Q4 and annual financial results with notable figures.

news.summary: Consumer confidence in the US has sharply declined, raising concerns about potential stagflation. The University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index dropped to 50.4 in April, its lowest since June 2022 and well below the expected 54.5. The current economic conditions subindex fell by 11.4% to 56.5, while consumer expectations dropped to 47.2, nearly 30 points below April 2024 levels. This marks the fourth consecutive monthly decline in consumer sentiment. The alarming data suggests growing unease among American households about the economic outlook. For the full article, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Consumer sentiment hits 3-year low with Consumer Sentiment Index at 50.4 in April, a steep drop from March's 57.

news.summary: In 2025, Bitcoin and gold are diverging in their trajectories. Gold has surpassed $3,160 per ounce, boosted by factors such as central bank acquisitions, a move away from the U.S. dollar, and its reputation as a reliable safe-haven investment. This spike in gold prices has attracted investors seeking stability amidst global uncertainty and fears of an economic downturn. Conversely, Bitcoin has faced challenges, dropping from a peak of over $80,000 to $77,000 in April - its lowest point of the year so far. This contrast in performance reflects the shifting macroeconomic landscape and geopolitical tensions influencing the two assets.
news.headline: Gold is exceeding Bitcoin in 2025, surpassing $3,160/oz amid global instability, while Bitcoin drops to $77,000 in April.

news.summary: The trade policies put forth by President Trump are expected to increase the cost of imports and create added complexity in determining and submitting tariffs.
news.headline: Trump's tariffs raise import costs, shifting burden onto consumers and affecting businesses, complicating tariff calculations and payments.

news.summary: Recent reports on inflation in the U.S. have failed to calm the Treasury market, with bond yields increasing again, leading to speculation that a policy misalignment could prompt action from the Federal Reserve. The Bureau of Labor Statistics revealed that the Producer Price Index (PPI) fell 0.4% in March, the largest drop seen since 2023. On an annual basis, producer inflation slowed to 2.7%, below the expected 3.3% and down from 3.2% in February. Core producer prices also fell, underscoring the weak inflation dynamics. Notable declines included eggs at -21.3%, fresh and dry vegetables at -13.0%, and fruits at -12.2%. Gasoline and diesel fuel prices also dropped. Despite cooling inflation, Treasury yields rose, with the 10-year yield hitting 4.50% and the 30-year yield reaching 4.92%, marking a 7 and 5 basis-point increase respectively.
news.headline: Inflation slows, yields rise, potential rate cut looms as PPI falls 0.4% in March, lowest since 2023; core prices drop 0.1% and 2.7% annually; CPI cools but bond yields climb - 10-year at 4.50%, 30-year at 4.92%.

news.summary: Philadelphia-based company Vital Start Health has announced plans to raise $3 million to expand its Pregnancy+ partnership. The company aims to scale its Virtual Reality enabled mental health services, which are covered by insurance.
news.headline: Vital Start Health Raises $3M to Expand Pregnancy+ VR Support Tools, aims to scale mental health services with insurance coverage.

news.summary: Merit Medical Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: MMSI) is set to report an adjusted EPS of 75 cents and sales totaling $352.82 million for the first quarter of 2025. The company specializes in developing, manufacturing, and distributing medical devices for various procedures such as cardiology, radiology, oncology, critical care, and endoscopy. According to Barrington Research, in 2024, approximately 41% of Merit Medical's $1.36 billion revenue came from international markets, with China alone contributing about 11% of the total revenue for the company in the same year. Analysts also note that the company has multiple manufacturing and packaging facilities located outside the U.S., including in Mexico and Brazil. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US-China trade tensions may impact Merit Medical's $150 million revenue from China; in 2024, 11% of its revenue came from China.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, Mandalay Resources Corporation shared positive first-quarter production results for the period concluding on March 31, 2025. The Company, known as Mandalay, reported strong performance with its stock listed as TSX: MND and OTCQB: MNDJF.
news.headline: Mandalay Resources meets Q1 2025 gold and antimony production targets per guidance. TSX: MND, OTCQB: MNDJF mentioned.

news.summary: During a recent CNBC interview, Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari expressed worries about changing investor sentiment towards the U.S., mentioning odd market behavior amidst trade tensions. Noteworthy is the sharp increase in Treasury yields and the simultaneous weakening of the dollar, a departure from the usual response to geopolitical instability. Kashkari noted that typically, when tariffs rise, the dollar strengthens; hence, the current decline in the dollar adds credibility to concerns. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Fed's Kashkari expresses concern: US losing appeal to global capital due to trade tensions. Dollar down, Treasury yields up.

news.summary: If reciprocal tariffs and an additional 50% tax on Chinese imports are implemented at midnight, the Trump tariffs will near the $1 trillion tax-increase mark. This development caused a drop in the S&P 500 index.
news.headline: Trump tariffs nearing $1 trillion tax hike with reciprocal tariffs & 50% tax on Chinese imports at midnight; S&P 500 drops.

news.summary: First Bancshares, Inc. (OTCQX: FBSI) based in Mountain Grove, Missouri, disclosed its quarterly financial results for the period ending March 31, 2025. The company, which owns Stockmens Bank, announced its unaudited figures.
news.headline: First Bancshares, Inc. reveals Q1 2025 results for Stockmens Bank in Mountain Grove, Mo. (OTCQX: FBSI), figures undisclosed.

news.summary: In the typically stable government bond market, there has been a notable increase in the yield on the 10-year Treasury. The yield has surged to approximately 4.5 percent, marking a significant rise from its previous level of less than 4 percent by the end of the previous week.
news.headline: Trump's tariffs disrupt bond market: 10-year Treasury yield jumps to 4.5% from under 4% last week.

news.summary: The Federal Reserve may need to intervene as Treasury yields surge, amidst a growing possibility of an economic downturn. The increase in Trump's tariffs is causing the 10-year Treasury yield to become unhinged, leading to a decline in the S&P 500. This development highlights a potential need for actions from the Federal Reserve to stabilize the situation.
news.headline: Fed may intervene as Treasury yields rise due to Trump tariffs, risking recession. Market affected: 10-yr yield, S&P 500.

news.summary: In New York, Wall Street bounced back from early losses, with major indexes turning positive during midday trading. Investors were pleased by strong bank earnings and lower-than-expected inflation data, largely overlooking worries about the trade war as China hiked tariffs on U.S. products to 125%. Producer prices in March cooled to 2.7% annually, well below the anticipated 3.3%, with a monthly drop of 0.4%, the most significant decline since 2023. Consumer prices also decreased more than predicted the day before. The latest University of Michigan consumer sentiment index plummeted to 50.4, the lowest since June 2022. Inflation expectations rose significantly, with short-term projections reaching levels not seen since 1990 and long-term views at levels last observed in 1981, highlighting concerns about persistent price pressures. The 30-year Treasury bond market... Visit Benzinga.com for the complete article.
news.headline: Wall Street rebounds on strong Q1 earnings like JPMorgan; China raises tariffs to 125%; inflation drops to 2.7% in March.
